sections.paragraph:
ἀδυνατέω fut. ἀδυνατήσω lit. ‘be powerless, be disabled’ (so since Epicharmus [? c. 480 b.c.], Fgm. 266, who uses it personally [Vorsokrat.5 23 B, 23 ἀδυνατεῖ δʼ οὐδὲν θεός]; also PEnteux 26, 3 [III b.c.]; UPZ 110, 13 and 89 [164 b.c.] 6, 36 [163 b.c.] al.; Herm. Wr. 10, 18; Philo; Jos., Ant. 15, 211; Ar. 3, 2; Tat. 16, 2; Ath. 21, 2; R. 57, 27); in NT only impers. (Philod., Ira p. 98 W.; Job 10:13; 42:2; 2 Ch 14:10; Wsd 13:16) it is impossible οὐδὲν ἀδυνατήσει ὑμῖν Mt 17:20; οὐκ ἀδ. παρὰ τοῦ θεοῦ (v.l. π. τῷ θεῷ) πᾶν ῥῆμα nothing will be impossible w. God (as far as God is concerned) Lk 1:37 (Gen 18:14; Dt 17:8.—The idea that nothing is impossible for gods is also found among Greeks from early times: Od. 16, 211f; Hes., Works 5f; Alcaeus 78, 7 Diehl [34, 7 L.-P.]).—DELG s.v. δύναμαι. M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

PEnteux PEnteux = Papyrus Enteuxeis—List 4

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

NT NT = New Testament

impers. impers. = impersonal(ly)

Philod Philod , I b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Od Od , s. Hom.; VIII? b.c.—List 5

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Alcaeus Alcaeus , lyric poet, VII–VI b.c.—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

  1. L. = KLachmann, editor of the Greek NT (1842–50)—List 1, beg.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 21
